human_verification.notes: Human review recorded for issue #1601 against manifest, research summary, functionality map, scenario suite, results, readiness, deployment, and commercialization artifacts.

notes: Source set covers NIST cybersecurity and cloud guidance, CISA operational cloud guidance, CIS controls and cloud benchmarks, CSA cloud control evidence, AWS vendor-official guidance, FedRAMP cloud evidence references, and the current package baseline.
Every manifest authority source records publisher, authority rationale, refresh interval, decay policy, and review date.
Scenario suite exceeds the minimum scenario count of 12.

Coverage includes normal in-scope analysis, partial evidence, prioritization, evidence-backed rationale, low-confidence escalation, adjacent-lane routing, formal refusal triggers, hold/release control, corrective-action escalation, conflicting policy handling, tenant adaptation, and package regression review.
All shared abilities, quality-safety-and-assurance workflow abilities, and cross-industry profile ability are declared and mapped to passing scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario coverage.
Research summary defines workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario family coverage, and nonblocking ambiguity handling.
Unresolved ambiguity is explicitly accepted only with runtime dependency or escalation rules.
Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #1601.
Runtime may remain specification-based until future frequent-use evidence, connector review, regression history, and human approval justify retained implementation promotion.

Out-of-scope boundaries explicitly reject live penetration testing, incident response execution, formal audit/compliance attestation, broad non-security cloud architecture ownership, hold/release bypass, and production approval.
